title: iberodes littoralis subsp. gallaecica
intro: Iberodes littoralis subsp. gallaecica, also known as Iberis gummifera, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Iberis gummifera include relieving coughs and congestion, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are traditionally used to address respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
